_note: Vendored ORDERED review methodology (the 'chain') the judgment subagent walks, per mode. This carries ONLY the step ordering + cross-cutting methodology — the per-risk symptom / 'do NOT flag' / source prose is NOT duplicated here; it derives from decay-risks.json at prompt-build time (CLAUDE.md rule 2). Risk codes here are mapped by SMELL NAME to decay-risks.json (e.g. T1=Test Obscurity, T5=Coverage Illusion), not by step position. Drift-tracked: scripts/check-drift verifies every step code is a real decay-risks code and that _source matches the pinned upstream rev. Spec 384 (quality-prose-templates) should DERIVE its per-mode guidance prose from this single source, not re-vendor it.

_methodology.iron_law: EVERY finding follows Symptom -> Source -> Consequence -> Remedy. A finding without a consequence AND a remedy is noise, not a finding -- drop it.
_methodology.severity: Critical = breaks velocity / creates production risk today; Warning = will degrade through the next few features if left; Suggestion = worth fixing when nearby, not urgent.
_methodology.scope_calibration: Skip generated files (protobuf stubs, OpenAPI clients, ORM migrations, lock files, minified bundles). For a large change, sample the highest-risk areas and say the review is sampled.
_methodology.fix_order: List Critical findings first; with more than five findings, end with a one-line recommended fix order.
_methodology.restraint: A threshold crossing is a hint, not a verdict. Honor each risk's 'do NOT flag' guard. Reply [] when nothing genuinely applies.

review.title: PR Review
review.purpose: Analyze a diff or files for decay risks directly visible in the changed code.

steps.name: Understand the scope
steps.intent: State the change's purpose and the files touched; a PR touching more than ~10 unrelated files is itself a Change Propagation signal.

steps.name: Scan for Change Propagation
steps.intent: Scan first -- most visible in a diff: cross-module edits beyond what the feature needs, divergent change, feature envy, inappropriate intimacy.

steps.name: Scan for Cognitive Overload
steps.intent: Long, deeply-nested or over-parameterised new functions; magic values; cryptic names; train-wreck chains.

steps.name: Scan for Knowledge Duplication
steps.intent: Logic, a concept name, or a class hierarchy that already exists elsewhere in the codebase.

steps.name: Scan for Accidental Complexity
steps.intent: One-use abstractions, pure wrappers/delegators, extension points serving no current requirement.

steps.name: Scan for Dependency Disorder
steps.intent: High-level modules importing low-level ones, new cycles, interfaces forcing unused dependencies. Skip if no new imports.

steps.name: Scan for Domain Model Distortion
steps.intent: Names that mismatch the business language; pure data bags where behavior was expected; domain logic stranded in a utility layer.

steps.name: Quick Test Check
steps.intent: Three signals only: new behavior shipped without tests (Coverage Illusion); mock setup longer than the test logic (Mock Abuse); vague test names with no assertion messages (Test Obscurity). Skip for docs/config-only changes.

audit.title: Architecture Audit
audit.purpose: Assess module structure, dependency direction, and domain fidelity across the codebase.

steps.name: Gather codebase context
steps.intent: Map the modules, entry points, and layering before judging any single edge.

steps.name: Draw the module dependency graph
steps.intent: Trace imports between modules; a cycle or a high-to-low edge is the audit's primary signal.

steps.name: Scan for Dependency Disorder
steps.intent: Dependencies flowing against the abstraction gradient; cycles; fat interfaces; fan-out above five.

steps.name: Scan for Domain Model Distortion
steps.intent: Anemic models, domain logic leaked into services/utilities, names that mismatch the domain.

steps.name: Scan the remaining risks
steps.intent: Cognitive overload, change propagation, knowledge duplication, and accidental complexity at module scope.

steps.name: Testability seam assessment
steps.intent: Can modules be tested in isolation, or do hard-wired dependencies block the seams (Architecture Mismatch)?

steps.name: Conway's Law check
steps.intent: Does the module structure mirror the team and communication structure, or fight it?

debt.title: Tech Debt Assessment
debt.purpose: Inventory decay across the codebase and rank it by remediation leverage.

steps.name: Full decay-risk scan
steps.intent: Scan all six code-decay risks across the scope -- breadth first, not depth.

steps.name: Score each finding by Pain x Spread
steps.intent: Pain = how much it hurts to work near it; Spread = how much code it touches. The product ranks leverage.

steps.name: Classify debt intent
steps.intent: Deliberate vs inadvertent, prudent vs reckless (Fowler's debt quadrant) -- frames remedy urgency.

steps.name: Group by decay risk
steps.intent: Cluster findings by risk so the report shows systemic patterns, not a flat list.

test.title: Test Quality Review
test.purpose: Diagnose test-suite decay -- the six test risks T1-T6.

steps.name: Scan for Test Obscurity
steps.intent: Vague test names; no assertion messages; the scenario and expected outcome are not expressed by the name.

steps.name: Scan for Test Brittleness
steps.intent: Tests coupled to implementation detail that break on a safe refactor.

steps.name: Scan for Mock Abuse
steps.intent: Mock setup longer than the test logic; assertions only that a mock was called, with no behavior verified.

steps.name: Scan for Test Duplication
steps.intent: The same scenario asserted many times; copy-pasted setup that should be a shared fixture.

steps.name: Scan for Coverage Illusion and Architecture Mismatch
steps.intent: Behavior that looks covered but is not (Coverage Illusion); a test structure that cannot reach the design's seams (Architecture Mismatch).

steps.name: Apply the Iron Law and output the report
steps.intent: Every test finding still needs Symptom -> Source -> Consequence -> Remedy.

health.title: Health Dashboard
health.purpose: A fast four-dimension health snapshot rather than a deep per-risk review.

steps.name: PR dimension
steps.intent: Abbreviated diff scan for Change Propagation and Cognitive Overload; cap at three findings.

steps.name: Architecture dimension
steps.intent: Dependency Disorder at module scope: circular deps, upward flows, fan-out above five.

steps.name: Debt dimension
steps.intent: All six code-decay risks, severity-tier only (skip Pain x Spread); cap at two per code, three overall.

steps.name: Test dimension
steps.intent: Test Obscurity and Test Brittleness across the test files; cap at three findings.

steps.name: Compute dashboard scores and output
steps.intent: Roll each dimension into a score; the dashboard plus the one or two highest-leverage actions is the deliverable, not a flat finding list.

sweep.title: Full Sweep
sweep.purpose: Run every mode's pass in sequence for a complete decay picture.

steps.name: Review pass (R1-R6 code decay)
steps.intent: The full PR-review chain over the scope.

steps.name: Test pass (T1-T6 test decay)
steps.intent: The full test-quality chain over the test suite.

steps.name: Debt pass
steps.intent: Rank the accumulated findings by Pain x Spread.

steps.name: Architecture pass
steps.intent: Dependency direction and domain fidelity at module scope.

steps.name: Aggregate residuals and report
steps.intent: De-duplicate across passes; produce one ranked report, Critical first.
